what_it_does: It is an interpretive design-consult memo from the projection build-loop to the lead (relaying Tim), answering questions about 'parent work' and multi-embedding in light of the default embedding switch from bge-1024 to pplx-2560. It audits the live vector store, diagnoses layer non-uniformity, and prescribes migration rules for retrieval, scale reads, and composition.
observations: The author performed a full scan of 5,852 vectors in .data/store/vectors and tabulated (space, emb, dim).
Main spaces are already non-uniform: common_knowledge is pplx-only; operators has a 2560-dim default anomaly; most others carry both bge-1024 and pplx-2560.
Scale pyramids are per-layer artifacts: repo and history pyramids are pplx-only, while principles/topics/worldview/operators still have parallel bge and pplx pyramids.
78 unspaced bge vectors with no source are identified as dead orphans that should be dropped.
Regression 3560163 is cited as a real failure caused by querying a scale pyramid at the wrong embedding layer, fixed by threading emb explicitly.
Cross-layer composition is framed as late fusion (RRF, score-blend, rerank), never vector concatenation, averaging, or MRL truncation across models.
A render proof is provided for common_knowledge using pplx, claiming the comprehend→publish→project→resolve circuit works end-to-end.
The document includes a migration checklist and a one-line governing rule about default as resolution, not collapse.
standouts: The vector store is already a non-uniform multi-layer landscape, not a clean single-default one.
The governing rule: a default resolves a variable but must not collapse the layer axis; multi-ness survives only if layers stay present and reads name emb explicitly.
Scale pyramids are literal per-layer parent hierarchies, so the pplx switch partially re-parented some spaces.
The dead-orphan diagnosis and the reranker-as-first-composition sequencing.
conventions: Evidence-first structure: tables of live vector counts before interpretation.
Q&A organization with numbered sections and a governing rule plus checklist.
Breaks the convention that a global default embedding is safe by treating omit-emb as a latent bug.
concerns: The author explicitly cannot resolve which 'parent work' Tim means and offers three guesses, so part of the document is speculative.
It contains forward-looking prescriptions and a migration checklist whose implementation status is not shown in the file.
Internal ticket/commit IDs (3560163, 1f88727, t-1781594751-ch-al7jdfdr) appear without surrounding context.
The operators default=2560 anomaly is noted but not explained.
The render proof date (2026-06-16) is future-dated relative to typical present, suggesting fictional or projected evidence.
notes: A self-aware consult memo that uses a live vector audit to argue the pplx default must not silently collapse the existing multi-layer embedding stack, and it prescribes explicit-layer reads plus late-fusion composition.
questions: Which of the three 'parent work' readings did Tim actually intend?
What code changes currently implement the explicit emb threading fix for scale reads?
Why does operators have a 2560-dim default while every other main space defaults to 1024?
Have the 78 unspaced orphan vectors actually been dropped, or only diagnosed?
How is the reranker toggle wired into consult/R2 retrieval paths?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is presented as projection's interpretive answer to Tim's question and it delivers both diagnosis of the embedding landscape and prescriptive migration constraints.
apparent_themes: Everything is a variable, nothing static (layer as a chosen lens).
Explicitness in read paths prevents silent cross-layer failures.
Late fusion and reranking before cross-layer vector merging.
Correctness of the base retrieval path before adding composition complexity.
intention_signals: It is trying to protect the multi-layer embedding architecture from being flattened by the pplx default switch, and to sequence retrieval improvements so single-layer correctness and reranking come before cross-layer fusion.
novelty: It treats embedding default as a loaded variable rather than a benign convenience, uses a live vector audit to prove the store is already non-uniform, and frames scale pyramids as per-layer parent hierarchies that must be queried at their build layer.
contribution: A concrete, evidence-backed map of which spaces carry which embedding layers and a governing rule for preserving multi-layer explicitness during a default-model migration.
summary_for_embedding: A consult memo documenting a 5,852-vector audit of embedding layers across main and scale spaces, interpreting parent-work and multi-embedding questions, and prescribing explicit-layer reads, late-fusion composition, and a migration checklist for a pplx-default retrieval system.
intention_for_embedding: To guide the lead and Tim in respecting the existing multi-layer embedding architecture while switching the default read layer to pplx.
